```json
{
  "name": "Fix YukYuks scraper parity",
  "overview": "Bring Yuk Yuk’s scraper in both current and legacy codepaths to parity with the recent Halifax Live robustness fixes for images, descriptions/excerpts, and date/time extraction.",
  "plan": "# Fix Yuk Yuk’s Scraper Reliability\n\n## Scope\n- Current pipeline scraper: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/yukyuks_scraper.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/yukyuks_scraper.py)\n- Legacy reference scraper: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/archive/legacy-scrapers/2026-04-23_182857/yukyuks_scraper_old.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/archive/legacy-scrapers/2026-04-23_182857/yukyuks_scraper_old.py)\n\n## Problems Confirmed\n- Output currently shows event rows with empty description/excerpt fields and default logo image for all rows.\n- Current extraction depends heavily on generic card-parent parsing and weak fallbacks, similar to Halifax Live pre-fix behavior.\n- Date/time extraction uses narrow regex patterns and should be hardened for additional format variants.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. Add a structured field extraction layer in the current Yuk Yuk’s scraper\n- Parse event card data with explicit priority order instead of relying on broad parent text.\n- Introduce helper functions for robust date-range, single-date, and time normalization.\n\n2. Improve image extraction and fallback hierarchy\n- Expand candidate image source order (src/data-src/data-lazy/srcset/background-image/closest relevant container image).\n- Validate URL quality and avoid selecting placeholders/icons before final fallback.\n- Keep the logo fallback only as final fallback when no event image exists.\n\n3. Improve description and excerpt extraction\n- Prefer event-specific descriptive text blocks in card content.\n- Filter boilerplate text (e.g., CTA/button labels and low-signal snippets).\n- Build excerpt from cleaned description consistently.\n\n4. Harden date and time parsing\n- Support additional time variants (&#96;8 PM&#96;, &#96;8:00PM&#96;, &#96;20:00&#96;, etc.).\n- Improve date range parsing and month/year rollover reliability.\n- Preserve existing output conventions while reducing blank/misparsed dates.\n\n5. Apply parity updates to the archived legacy Yuk Yuk’s scraper\n- Mirror key helper improvements where practical so the older codepath remains usable for fallback/debugging runs.\n\n6. Verify outputs with targeted checks\n- Run Yuk Yuk’s scraper and validate CSV coverage metrics:\n  - non-default featured images increase\n  - description/excerpt coverage increases\n  - date/time coverage remains correct or improves\n- Sanity-check syntax/lint for modified files.\n\n## Verification Focus\n- &#96;EVENT FEATURED IMAGE&#96; no longer defaults to logo for most rows when page images are available.\n- &#96;EVENT DESCRIPTION&#96; and &#96;EVENT EXCERPT&#96; are populated for the majority of events.\n- &#96;EVENT START DATE&#96; / &#96;EVENT START TIME&#96; parsing remains stable across single-day and multi-day events.",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Refactor current Yuk Yuk’s scraper parsing helpers and extraction priority for image/description/date/time",
      "id": "add-yukyuks-structured-extraction",
      "status": "pending"
    },

      "content": "Harden Yuk Yuk’s date/time parsing for additional variants and range handling",

      "status": "pending"

    },
    {
      "content": "Run scraper and validate output field coverage plus syntax/lint checks",
      "id": "verify-yukyuks-output-quality",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented the plan end-to-end and completed all existing to-dos in order.

Updated `yukyuks_scraper.py` to make extraction much more reliable:
- Added helper-based extraction for:
  - image URL normalization/validation
  - best card image selection (`src`, lazy attrs, `srcset`, background-image)
  - better description selection from card content
  - hardened date/time parsing (`8 PM`, `8:00PM`, `20:00`, ranges)
- Added detail-page enrichment (`fetch_event_details`) to pull:
  - event-specific description
  - `og:image` / `twitter:image` (with fallback scanning)
- Wired `parse_event_card()` to use card extraction first, then detail-page enrichment, then fallback logo only as last resort.
- Kept existing output schema and behavior conventions intact.

Updated legacy parity file `archive/legacy-scrapers/2026-04-23_182857/yukyuks_scraper_old.py`:
- Hardened time parsing to support more formats.
- Added event image extraction near ticket links (with placeholder filtering).
- Improved excerpt sizing consistency.
- Filled `EVENT FEATURED IMAGE` with extracted image or logo fallback.

Verification performed:
- Ran current scraper: `python3 yukyuks_scraper.py`
- Current output coverage (`general scraper/output/yukyuks_events.csv`):
  - `rows`: 6
  - `with_image`: 6
  - `non_logo_images`: 6
  - `with_description`: 6
  - `with_excerpt`: 6
  - `with_start_date`: 6
  - `with_start_time`: 6

- Legacy output coverage:
  - `rows`: 10
  - `with_description`: 10
  - `with_excerpt`: 10
  - `with_start_time`: 10
  - `with_image`: 10
  - `non_logo_images`: 0
- Syntax check passed for both files (`py_compile`).
- Lint check returned no errors for edited files.

If you want, I can also add lightweight `[sources]` telemetry logging to Yuk Yuk’s (like Halifax Live) so future regressions are easier to spot quickly.

_**Agent (default)**_

Done — I added source telemetry to `yukyuks_scraper.py` and confirmed it runs cleanly.

Why legacy found 10 rows while current found 6:

- **Current scraper intentionally limits scope to next 30 days** in `scrape_yukyuks()` via `cutoff_date = today + timedelta(days=30)` and skips beyond-cutoff rows.
- **Current scraper deduplicates across months** using `EVENT WEBSITE + EVENT START DATE`, so repeated listings between month pages collapse to one.
- **Current scraper also explicitly skips placeholder events** like “Comics to be announced”.
- **Legacy scraper does none of that**:
  - no 30-day window filter
  - no cross-month dedupe
  - parses all detected “Book Tickets” blocks from a single page scrape

So the row count difference is expected behavior, not a bug: legacy is “wider/unfiltered”, current is “production filtered”.

Also, telemetry now prints per event like:
- `time=parsed|missing`
- `image=detail|card|default_logo`
- `description=detail|card|missing`

From the latest run, all kept rows were `image=detail` and `description=detail`, which is exactly what we wanted.

---

Short answer: **not difficult**.

From a quick scan, the only active hardcoded “30 days out” limit I found is in `hfx-now/yukyuks_scraper.py` (and an archived old file). Most other scrapers appear to rely on source site availability rather than a shared global day-window cap.

So complexity is:

- **Minimal** if you want a direct fix now: change `30` → `90` in Yuk Yuk’s.
- **Small-to-moderate** if you want this standardized “for all scrapers”: add one shared setting (e.g., `LOOKAHEAD_DAYS = 90`) and have each scraper that uses date filtering read from it.

If you want, I can do the better long-term version now:

Done — I completed the sweep and aligned remaining window-capped logic to your “all future events” direction.

### What I changed

- Updated active `yukyuks_scraper.py` wording so it no longer claims “next 30 days”.
- Updated archived `yukyuks_scraper_old_requests.py` behavior from 30-day cap to:
  - keep `today+` events (no upper day cap),
  - crawl forward by month with safe stop conditions,
  - dedupe by `EVENT WEBSITE + EVENT START DATE`.

### What I confirmed

- Codebase scan shows no other active scraper hardcoded to 30/60/90-day future caps.
- Merge already keeps all future events by default (`SKIP_PAST_EVENTS=True`, no upper-day cutoff).
- Validation checks passed:
  - `py_compile` on changed files: pass
  - lints on changed files: clean
  - current Yuk Yuk’s output still shows future range:
    - `rows: 15`
    - `min_date: 2026-04-24`
    - `max_date: 2026-11-26`

If you want, next I can add a single shared “event horizon policy” config file (`past-only skip`, `future-only`, optional max-days) so this is explicit and easy to control project-wide.

_**Agent (default)**_

Excellent — I implemented the shared policy layer.

### What I added

- New shared module: `hfx-now/event_horizon.py`
  - `SKIP_PAST_EVENTS = True`
  - `MAX_FUTURE_DAYS = None` (this means **all future events**, no upper cap)
  - `is_within_event_horizon(date_str)` helper
  - Shared month-crawl safety constants:
    - `MONTH_CRAWL_MAX_MONTHS`
    - `MONTH_CRAWL_EMPTY_STOP`

### Where I wired it

- `hfx-now/yukyuks_scraper.py`
  - Uses shared horizon helper for keep/skip decisions.
  - Uses shared month-crawl safety constants.
- `hfx-now/merge_master_events.py`
  - Uses shared `SKIP_PAST_EVENTS` + horizon helper.
- `hfx-now/merge_master_events_v3.py`
  - Uses shared `SKIP_PAST_EVENTS` + horizon helper.
- `hfx-now/propeller_scraper.py`
  - Future filtering now uses shared horizon helper.
- `hfx-now/make_titles_unique.py`
  - “Filter out past events” step now uses shared horizon helper.

### Why this helps

Now your future-event policy is defined once.  
If you ever want to reintroduce a cap (e.g., 180 days), you can set:

- `MAX_FUTURE_DAYS = 180` in `event_horizon.py`

…and all wired scripts will follow it.

### Verification run

- `py_compile` passed on all changed files.
- Shared policy sanity check returned:
  - `skip_past: True`
  - `max_future_days: None`
  - today date included, past date excluded
- Lints are clean on edited files.

If you want, next I can run one full `master_runner_v3.py` + `merge_master_events_v3.py` pass and give you a before/after row-count delta by source.

```json
  "plan": "# Fix Propeller Venue + Showpass Time Quality\n\n## Scope\n- Propeller scraper: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/propeller_scraper.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/propeller_scraper.py)\n- Showpass scraper: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/showpass_halifax_scraper.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/showpass_halifax_scraper.py)\n- Merge guardrails: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/merge_master_events.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/merge_master_events.py) and [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/merge_master_events_v3.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/merge_master_events_v3.py)\n- Theme/import venue fallback path: [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php)\n\n## Root Causes Found\n- Propeller venue names exist in scraper output, but frontend shows &#96;Venue TBA&#96; when event-to-venue relationship is unresolved in payload generation (&#96;hfx_event_to_payload()&#96; falls back to TBA).\n- Showpass scraper currently captures full date/time lines (e.g. &#96;Starts: 9:00 PM ADT&#96;) and writes them as &#96;EVENT START TIME&#96;; merge dedupe normalizes times for matching but export values can remain malformed.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. **Propeller hardening in scraper layer**\n- Ensure Propeller rows consistently include rich venue fields (name/address/city/province/country/zip if available in source mapping).\n- Preserve existing venue naming conventions while improving importer matching chances.\n\n2. **Propeller direct fix in import/theme layer**\n- Update venue resolution logic in &#96;hfx_event_to_payload()&#96; to use a reliable fallback path before &#96;Venue TBA&#96; (e.g., venue text/meta fallback when &#96;_EventVenueID&#96; is missing).\n- Keep behavior backward-compatible for events with properly linked TEC venues.\n\n3. **Showpass source parsing fix**\n- Refactor Showpass time extraction to parse explicit start/end clock tokens from mixed strings (&#96;Starts:&#96;, &#96;@ 6:30 PM ADT&#96;, &#96;7PM Doors / 8PM Showtime&#96;, ranges).\n- Normalize to project time format before writing scraper CSV fields.\n\n4. **Merge normalization guardrail (defense-in-depth)**\n- Apply robust &#96;Event Start Time&#96;/&#96;Event End Time&#96; normalization in merge row normalization (not only dedupe comparison).\n- Keep invalid/unparseable values empty rather than carrying raw sentence fragments.\n\n5. **Verification and regression checks**\n- Run targeted scraper outputs for Propeller and Showpass.\n- Run merge(s) and verify:\n  - Propeller events render with non-TBA venue names.\n  - Showpass times are canonical and no longer prefixed with labels/full date strings.\n  - No regressions in existing source row counts and dedupe behavior.\n\n## Verification Focus\n- Propeller rows in merged/site payload no longer default to &#96;Venue TBA&#96; when venue text exists.\n- Showpass &#96;EVENT START TIME&#96; values are clean clock values across known problematic patterns.\n- Merge outputs remain stable and schema-compatible for imports.",
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 merge_master_events_v3.py && python3 -c "import csv,re,json; from collections import Counter; p1='output/propeller_events.csv'; p2='output/showpass_halifax_for_import.csv'; p3='output_v3/master_events_v3.csv';
prop=list(csv.DictReader(open(p1,encoding='utf-8'))); shp=list(csv.DictReader(open(p2,encoding='utf-8'))); m=list(csv.DictReader(open(p3,encoding='utf-8')));
prop_tba=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE NAME') or '').strip().lower() in {'','tba','venue tba'});
prop_city=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE CITY') or '').strip());
prop_addr=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE ADDRESS') or '').strip());
raw_bad=sum(1 for r in shp if re.search(r'(starts:|@|\||\b[a-z]{3,9}\s+\d{1,2})', (r.get('EVENT START TIME') or '').lower()));
merged_showpass=[r for r in m if (r.get('SOURCE') or '').strip().lower()=='showpasshalifax'];
merged_bad=sum(1 for r in merged_showpass if re.search(r'(starts:|@|\||\b[a-z]{3,9}\s+\d{1,2})', (r.get('Event Start Time') or '').lower()));
print(json.dumps({'propeller_rows':len(prop),'propeller_tba_venues':prop_tba,'propeller_with_city':prop_city,'propeller_with_address':prop_addr,'showpass_rows':len(shp),'showpass_raw_time_bad_pattern_rows':raw_bad,'merged_v3_rows':len(m),'merged_showpass_rows':len(merged_showpass),'merged_showpass_bad_time_pattern_rows':merged_bad},indent=2))"</summary>

```bash
python3 merge_master_events_v3.py && python3 -c "import csv,re,json; from collections import Counter; p1='output/propeller_events.csv'; p2='output/showpass_halifax_for_import.csv'; p3='output_v3/master_events_v3.csv';
prop=list(csv.DictReader(open(p1,encoding='utf-8'))); shp=list(csv.DictReader(open(p2,encoding='utf-8'))); m=list(csv.DictReader(open(p3,encoding='utf-8')));
prop_tba=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE NAME') or '').strip().lower() in {'','tba','venue tba'});
prop_city=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE CITY') or '').strip());
prop_addr=sum(1 for r in prop if (r.get('EVENT VENUE ADDRESS') or '').strip());
raw_bad=sum(1 for r in shp if re.search(r'(starts:|@|\||\b[a-z]{3,9}\s+\d{1,2})', (r.get('EVENT START TIME') or '').lower()));
merged_showpass=[r for r in m if (r.get('SOURCE') or '').strip().lower()=='showpasshalifax'];
merged_bad=sum(1 for r in merged_showpass if re.search(r'(starts:|@|\||\b[a-z]{3,9}\s+\d{1,2})', (r.get('Event Start Time') or '').lower()));
print(json.dumps({'propeller_rows':len(prop),'propeller_tba_venues':prop_tba,'propeller_with_city':prop_city,'propeller_with_address':prop_addr,'showpass_rows':len(shp),'showpass_raw_time_bad_pattern_rows':raw_bad,'merged_v3_rows':len(m),'merged_showpass_rows':len(merged_showpass),'merged_showpass_bad_time_pattern_rows':merged_bad},indent=2))"
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented the full plan and completed all to-dos in order.

What I changed:

- Updated `propeller_scraper.py`
  - Added richer venue fields directly to output:
    - `EVENT VENUE COUNTRY`
    - `EVENT VENUE STATE/PROVINCE`
    - `EVENT VENUE CITY`
    - `EVENT VENUE ADDRESS`
    - `EVENT VENUE ZIP`
  - Filled real taproom address/postal/city/province/country in `VENUE_INFO`.
  - Wired those fields into each row so importer/merge has stronger venue matching data.

- Updated theme fallback in `wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php`
  - In `hfx_event_to_payload()`, added venue-name meta fallback before `Venue TBA`.
  - It now checks common keys like `Event Venue Name`, `EVENT VENUE NAME`, etc., if `_EventVenueID`/`tribe_get_venue` don’t resolve.

- Updated `showpass_halifax_scraper.py`
  - Reworked time parsing to extract actual time tokens from mixed strings:
    - `Starts: 9:00 PM ADT`
    - `Fri Oct 3 @ 6:30 PM ADT`
    - `7PM Doors / 8PM Showtime`
  - `EVENT START TIME` / `EVENT END TIME` now store normalized clock values, not whole sentence fragments.

- Updated merge guardrails
  - `merge_master_events.py`: improved `normalize_time()` to extract clock tokens from noisy strings and return empty for unparseable values; applied normalization in `normalize_row_for_master()`.
  - `merge_master_events_v3.py`: same normalization improvements and applied in `normalize_row_for_v3()`.

Verification:

- Ran:
  - `python3 propeller_scraper.py`
  - `python3 showpass_halifax_scraper.py`
  - `python3 merge_master_events_v3.py`
- `merge_master_events.py` is still blocked by pre-existing environment issue:
  - `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scraper_paths'`
- Validation metrics:
  - Propeller (`output/propeller_events.csv`):
    - `propeller_rows: 6`
    - `propeller_tba_venues: 0`
    - `propeller_with_city: 6`
    - `propeller_with_address: 6`
  - Newly scraped Showpass output (`../output/showpass_halifax_for_import.csv`):
    - `showpass_rows_new_output: 12`
    - `bad_pattern_rows: 0`
  - Merged v3 (`output_v3/master_events_v3.csv`), Showpass rows:
    - `merged_showpass_bad_time_pattern_rows: 0`
- Lints: clean on all edited files.

One important note:
- `showpass_halifax_scraper.py` writes to `../output/showpass_halifax_for_import.csv` (parent output dir), while v3 merge currently reads `hfx-now/output/showpass_halifax_for_import.csv`. So your freshest Showpass scrape may not be the one merge consumes unless that path mismatch is addressed. I can fix that next if you want.

import re, pathlib, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base = pathlib.Path('.').resolve()
mism = []
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
    p = pathlib.Path(cfg.script)
    if not p.exists():
        p = base / p.name
    txt = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore')
    expected = pathlib.Path(cfg.output).name
    m = re.search(r'(?:CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*os\.path\.join\(OUTPUT_DIR,\s*[fr]?"([^"]+)"\)', txt)
    if not m:
        m = re.search(r"(?:CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*os\.path\.join\(OUTPUT_DIR,\s*[fr]?'([^']+)'\)", txt)
    actual = m.group(1) if m else None
    if actual and actual != expected:
        mism.append({'key': cfg.key, 'script': p.name, 'expected': expected, 'actual': actual})
    if actual is None:
        mism.append({'key': cfg.key, 'script': p.name, 'expected': expected, 'actual': 'UNPARSED'})
print(json.dumps({'count': len(mism), 'mismatches': mism}, indent=2))
PY</summary>

```bash
import re, pathlib, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base = pathlib.Path('.').resolve()
mism = []
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
    p = pathlib.Path(cfg.script)
    if not p.exists():
        p = base / p.name
    txt = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore')
    expected = pathlib.Path(cfg.output).name
    m = re.search(r'(?:CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*os\.path\.join\(OUTPUT_DIR,\s*[fr]?"([^"]+)"\)', txt)
    if not m:
        m = re.search(r"(?:CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*os\.path\.join\(OUTPUT_DIR,\s*[fr]?'([^']+)'\)", txt)
    actual = m.group(1) if m else None
    if actual and actual != expected:
        mism.append({'key': cfg.key, 'script': p.name, 'expected': expected, 'actual': actual})
    if actual is None:
        mism.append({'key': cfg.key, 'script': p.name, 'expected': expected, 'actual': 'UNPARSED'})
print(json.dumps({'count': len(mism), 'mismatches': mism}, indent=2))
```

from pathlib import Path
import scraper_registry as sr,re
base=Path('.').resolve()
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
    p=Path(cfg.script)
    if not p.exists(): p=base/p.name
    txt=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
    m=re.search(r'^(CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*(.+)$',txt,re.M)
    if m:
        print(f"{cfg.key:14} {p.name:32} {m.group(2).strip()}")
    else:
        print(f"{cfg.key:14} {p.name:32} <none>")
```bash
PY
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base=Path('.').resolve()
mism=[]
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
    p=Path(cfg.script)
    if not p.exists(): p=base/p.name
    txt=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
    expected=Path(cfg.output).name
    m=re.search(r'^(CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*(.+)$',txt,re.M)
    if not m:
        continue
    rhs=m.group(2).strip()
    # extract quoted filename if present
    name=None
    q=re.search(r'"([^"]+\.csv)"',rhs) or re.search(r"'([^']+\.csv)'",rhs)
    if q: name=Path(q.group(1)).name
    if name and name!=expected:
        mism.append({'key':cfg.key,'script':p.name,'expected':expected,'actual':name,'rhs':rhs})
print(json.dumps({'count':len(mism),'mismatches':mism},indent=2))
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base=Path('.').resolve()
mism=[]
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
    p=Path(cfg.script)
    if not p.exists(): p=base/p.name
    txt=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
    expected=Path(cfg.output).name
    m=re.search(r'^(CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*(.+)$',txt,re.M)
    if not m:
        continue
    rhs=m.group(2).strip()
    # extract quoted filename if present
    name=None
    q=re.search(r'"([^"]+\.csv)"',rhs) or re.search(r"'([^']+\.csv)'",rhs)
    if q: name=Path(q.group(1)).name
    if name and name!=expected:
        mism.append({'key':cfg.key,'script':p.name,'expected':expected,'actual':name,'rhs':rhs})
print(json.dumps({'count':len(mism),'mismatches':mism},indent=2))
PY
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base=Path('.').resolve()
mism=[]
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
 p=Path(cfg.script)
 if not p.exists(): p=base/p.name
 txt=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
 expected=Path(cfg.output).name
 m=re.search(r'^(CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*(.+)$',txt,re.M)
 if not m:
  continue
 rhs=m.group(2).strip()
 q=re.search(r'"([^"]+\.csv)"',rhs) or re.search(r"'([^']+\.csv)'",rhs)
 if not q:
  continue
 actual=Path(q.group(1)).name
 if actual!=expected:
  mism.append({'key':cfg.key,'script':p.name,'expected':expected,'actual':actual,'rhs':rhs})
print(json.dumps({'count':len(mism),'mismatches':mism},indent=2))
PY</summary>

```bash

from pathlib import Path
import re, json
import scraper_registry as sr
base=Path('.').resolve()
mism=[]
for cfg in sr.get_enabled_scrapers():
 p=Path(cfg.script)
 if not p.exists(): p=base/p.name
 txt=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
 expected=Path(cfg.output).name
 m=re.search(r'^(CSV_FILE|OUTPUT_CSV)\s*=\s*(.+)$',txt,re.M)
 if not m:
  continue
 rhs=m.group(2).strip()
 q=re.search(r'"([^"]+\.csv)"',rhs) or re.search(r"'([^']+\.csv)'",rhs)
 if not q:
  continue
 actual=Path(q.group(1)).name
 if actual!=expected:
  mism.append({'key':cfg.key,'script':p.name,'expected':expected,'actual':actual,'rhs':rhs})
print(json.dumps({'count':len(mism),'mismatches':mism},indent=2))
PY
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Great call — I addressed the Showpass mismatch and did a broader uniformity pass.

### What I fixed

- Added a shared path module: `hfx-now/scraper_paths.py`
  - Centralizes scraper output location via `OUTPUT_DIR` and `out_csv()`.
  - Default now points to the shared project output directory:
    - `.../general scraper/output`
  - Supports optional override with `HFX_SCRAPER_OUTPUT_DIR`.

- Normalized key scrapers to use shared output path
  - `showpass_halifax_scraper.py`
  - `yukyuks_scraper.py`
  - `propeller_scraper.py`
  - `discover_halifax_scraper.py`
  - `downtown_halifax_scraper.py`
  - `mma_scraper.py`

- Fixed Discover filename mismatch to align with registry
  - now outputs `discover_halifax_for_import.csv` (matching `scraper_registry.py`).

### Validation results

- `merge_master_events` import now works (no missing `scraper_paths` module).
- Path consistency check across active scrapers (registry expected vs scraper output basename):  
  - **0 mismatches**
- Key runtime path check now confirms these all write to the same shared output directory:
  - Showpass, Yuk Yuk’s, Propeller, Discover, Downtown, MMA
- `py_compile` passed for all touched files.
- Lints are clean.

---

### Suggestions to make this even more streamlined

1. **Migrate all active scrapers to `scraper_paths` explicitly**  
   (some already do, some still rely on local base-dir patterns).  
   This avoids future drift.

2. **Add a CI-style “path consistency check” script**  
   that compares `scraper_registry` expected outputs vs each scraper’s declared output file and fails fast on mismatch.

3. **Standardize `SOURCE` keys centrally**  
   to avoid subtle merge/source-key fragmentation (e.g., capitalization/spacing variations).

4. **Optional: one shared “normalizers” module**  
   for time/date/cost cleanup used by scrapers and merges, so behavior is consistent in one place.

If you want, I can implement #1 + #2 next in one pass.

---

    p=base/fn
    t=p.read_text()
    orig=t
    if 'from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR' not in t:
        # insert import after other local imports
        m=re.search(r'^(from .*\n)+(?!from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR)', t, flags=re.M)
        if m:
            # add after last consecutive from-import block near top
            # find insertion near first blank line after imports
            top=t.split('\n')
            ins=0
            for i,line in enumerate(top[:80]):
                if line.startswith('from ') or line.startswith('import '):
                    ins=i+1
            top.insert(ins,'from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR')
            t='\n'.join(top)
    t=t.replace('BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "output")\nos.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n',
                'os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n')
    t=t.replace("BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'output')\nos.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n",
                'os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n')
    # keep BASE_DIR if used for other purposes; remove only exact block
    if t!=orig:
        p.write_text(t)
        changed.append(fn)
print('\n'.join(changed))

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
base=Path('/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Halifax Now/Scrapers/general scraper/hfx-now')
files=[
'art_gallery_ns_scraper.py','bearlys_scraper.py','blue_mountain_friends_scraper.py','busstop_scraper.py','candlelight_scraper.py','carbonarc_scraper.py','dal_artgallery_scraper.py','goodrobot_scraper.py','gottingen_2037_scraper.py','halifax_matchmaker_scraper.py','hike_nova_scotia_scraper.py','jump_comedy_playwright_scraper.py','lighthouse_scraper.py','neptune_scraper.py','rumours_hfx_scraper.py','sanctuary_scraper.py','st_andrews_scraper.py','symphony_scraper.py','the_carleton_scraper.py','ticketmaster_scraper.py']
changed=[]
for fn in files:
    p=base/fn
    t=p.read_text()
    orig=t
    if 'from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR' not in t:
        # insert import after other local imports
        m=re.search(r'^(from .*\n)+(?!from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR)', t, flags=re.M)
        if m:
            # add after last consecutive from-import block near top
            # find insertion near first blank line after imports
            top=t.split('\n')
            ins=0
            for i,line in enumerate(top[:80]):
                if line.startswith('from ') or line.startswith('import '):
                    ins=i+1
            top.insert(ins,'from scraper_paths import OUTPUT_DIR')
            t='\n'.join(top)
    t=t.replace('BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "output")\nos.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n',
                'os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n')
    t=t.replace("BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'output')\nos.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n",
                'os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n')
    # keep BASE_DIR if used for other purposes; remove only exact block
    if t!=orig:
        p.write_text(t)
        changed.append(fn)
print('\n'.join(changed))
```

Done — I implemented both requested improvements.

### What I changed

- Standardized active scraper output paths onto shared `scraper_paths`:
  - Added/fixed usage in key scrapers (`showpass`, `yukyuks`, `propeller`, `discover`, `downtown`, `mma`).
  - Migrated remaining enabled scrapers to import shared `OUTPUT_DIR` so path behavior is uniform.

- Fixed concrete mismatches:
  - **Showpass path mismatch** (now writes to shared output directory expected by registry/merge).
  - **Discover filename mismatch** (`discover_halifax_for_import.csv`).
  - **Downtown + MMA path style mismatches** to shared `OUTPUT_DIR` join pattern.

- Added automated consistency checker:
  - New script: `hfx-now/tools/check_scraper_output_consistency.py`
  - Validates enabled registry entries against scraper output declarations and shared-path usage.

### Verification

- Consistency checker result:
  - `python3 tools/check_scraper_output_consistency.py` → `{"ok": true, "issues": []}`
- Compile checks:
  - `py_compile` passes for checker and updated modules.
- Lint checks:
  - No linter errors on edited files.

### Recommended ongoing workflow

Use this quick pre-run guard before full scrapes:

- `python3 tools/check_scraper_output_consistency.py`

If it returns non-zero or issues, fix those first; it will prevent silent merge gaps from path drift.

If you want, next I can add this checker as a first step inside `master_runner.py` / `master_runner_v3.py` so it runs automatically before scraping.

```
70:def fetch_html(url: str) -&gt; str:
71:    headers = {
72:        "User-Agent": (
73:            "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "
74:            "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
75:            "Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36"
76:        )
77:    }
78:    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30, verify=False)
79:    resp.raise_for_status()
80:    return resp.text
81:
82:
83:def get_event_links_from_index(html: str) -&gt; List[str]:
84:    """Collect all /event/... links from the events index page."""
85:    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
86:    links = set()
87:
88:    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
89:        href = a["href"]
90:        if "/event/" in href:
91:            links.add(urljoin(BASE_URL, href))
92:
93:    return sorted(links)
94:
95:
96:# ----------------------------
97:# Date / time parsing
98:# ----------------------------
99:
100:def parse_date_time(date_text: str, time_text: str):
101:    """
102:    Handle things like:
103:      'December 2, 2025 to January 27, 2026'
104:      'November 22, 2025'
105:      '1 pm to 3 pm'
106:    """
107:    date_text = " ".join(date_text.split())
108:    time_text = " ".join(time_text.replace("–", "-").split())
109:
110:    start_date_str = ""
111:    end_date_str = ""
112:
113:    # --- Dates ---
114:    if date_text:
115:        parts = re.split(r"\bto\b", date_text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
116:        start_part = parts[0].strip()
117:        end_part = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) &gt; 1 else start_part
118:
119:        try:
120:            start_dt = dateparser.parse(start_part, fuzzy=True)
121:            start_date_str = start_dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
122:        except Exception:
123:            start_date_str = ""
124:
125:        try:
126:            end_dt = dateparser.parse(end_part, fuzzy=True)
127:            end_date_str = end_dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
128:        except Exception:
129:            end_date_str = start_date_str
130:
131:    # --- Times ---
132:    start_time_str = ""
133:    end_time_str = ""
134:
135:    if time_text:
136:        # explicit range: "1 pm to 3 pm" or "1 pm - 3 pm"
137:        m = re.search(
138:            r"(\d{1,2}(?::\d{2})?\s*(?:am|pm))\s*(?:-|to)\s*(\d{1,2}(?::\d{2})?\s*(?:am|pm))",
139:            time_text,
140:            flags=re.IGNORECASE,
141:        )
142:        if m:
143:            start_time_str = to_24h(m.group(1))
144:            end_time_str = to_24h(m.group(2))
145:        else:
146:            m2 = re.search(r"(\d{1,2}(?::\d{2})?\s*(?:am|pm))", time_text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
147:            if m2:
148:                start_time_str = to_24h(m2.group(1))
149:
150:    if start_date_str and not end_date_str:
151:        end_date_str = start_date_str
152:
153:    return start_date_str, start_time_str, end_date_str, end_time_str
154:
155:
156:def to_24h(s: str) -&gt; str:
157:    s = s.strip().replace(".", "")
158:    try:
159:        dt = dateparser.parse(s, fuzzy=True)
160:        return dt.strftime("%H:%M")
161:    except Exception:
162:        return ""
163:
164:
165:# ----------------------------
166:# Event page parsing
167:# ----------------------------
168:
169:def parse_event_page(html: str, url: str) -&gt; Optional[Dict[str, str]]:
170:    """
171:    Use a very simple approach:
172:      - Title = &lt;h1&gt; text
173:      - Date line = first line anywhere on the page that looks like a date
174:      - Time line = first line after that with 'am' or 'pm'
175:      - Description = all text after the time line until 'Improve Your Experience'
176:      - Featured image = first &lt;img&gt; after the &lt;h1&gt;
177:    """
178:    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
179:
180:    h1 = soup.find("h1")
181:    if not h1:
182:        return None
183:    title = h1.get_text(strip=True)
184:
185:    # Collect all text lines from the page
186:    all_lines = [t.strip() for t in soup.get_text("\n", strip=True).split("\n") if t.strip()]
187:
188:    # Find date line index
189:    date_idx = None
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

They’re related, but each serves a different stage in the pipeline:

- `output/master_events.csv`  
  - **Current run’s full merged dataset** from all enabled scrapers (after normalization + dedupe).
  - Rebuilt each run.

- `output/events_archive.csv`  
  - **Historical cumulative archive** of all events ever seen across runs.
  - Updated each run by combining prior archive + current master, then deduping.
  - Used to determine what is “new” this run.

- `output/ready_to_import/new_events.csv`  
  - **Delta file**: only events in current `master_events.csv` that were **not** in `events_archive.csv` before this run.
  - Best when you only want to import newly discovered events.

- `output/ready_to_import/master_events.csv`  
  - **Import-location copy** of `output/master_events.csv` (same rows/columns, copied for workflow convenience).
  - Exists so import tooling can consistently pull from `ready_to_import/`.

In short:  
- `master_events.csv` = current truth  
- `events_archive.csv` = long-term memory  
- `ready_to_import/new_events.csv` = this run’s new additions  
- `ready_to_import/master_events.csv` = import-ready copy of current truth

Done — v2 now auto-populates `hfx_*` fields just like v3.

### What I changed
In `merge_master_events.py`, I backported the enrichment layer and wired it into normalization:

- Added enrichment helpers:
  - `strip_html`, `first_sentence`, `clip`
  - `infer_neighbourhood`, `infer_moods`, `infer_critic_pick`
  - `enrich_hfx_fields`
- Updated `normalize_row_for_master()` to return `enrich_hfx_fields(normalized)`.
- (From prior step) `hfx_*` columns are included in v2 schema/export headers.

### Verification
After running `python3 merge_master_events.py`, `output/ready_to_import/master_events.csv` now has populated `hfx_*` values:

- `hfx_short_blurb`: 364 rows
- `hfx_editor_blurb`: 364 rows
- `hfx_neighbourhood`: 476 rows
- `hfx_moods`: 444 rows
- `hfx_critic_pick`: 484 rows

So those fields are now both **present** and **auto-filled** in v2 ready-to-import output.

_**Agent (default)**_

You were right to be confused — there was a split-brain behavior in v3.

I fixed v3 so it now behaves as a true lane:

- v3 registry now tracks:
  - `source_output` = where scrapers currently write (`output/...`)
  - `output` = v3 canonical path (`output_v3/...`)
- `master_runner_v3.py` now mirrors each scraper CSV from `source_output` to `output_v3` immediately after each scraper run.
- `merge_master_events_v3.py` now reads via v3 registry paths (`output_v3/...`) and writes merged artifacts to `output_v3/...` as before.

### What this means now
- Running v3 will populate and consume v3 files end-to-end.
- Old `output/` may still get raw scraper files (because scraper scripts themselves still write there), but v3 pipeline logic now standardizes on `output_v3`.

### Verification I ran
- Confirmed all v3 registry `output` paths are under `output_v3`.
- Ran one scraper (`downtown`) through v3 runner code path:
  - source written in `output/...`
  - mirrored file created in `output_v3/downtown_halifax_for_import.csv`
- Ran `merge_master_events_v3.py` and confirmed it reads from `output_v3` and writes:
  - `output_v3/master_events_v3.csv`
  - `output_v3/events_archive_v3.csv`
  - `output_v3/ready_to_import_v3/new_events_v3.csv`
  - `output_v3/ready_to_import_v3/master_events_v3.csv`

Some scrapers showed `SKIP (no output CSV in output_v3)` only because they haven’t yet been mirrored in this session. A full `python3 master_runner_v3.py` run will fill those.

If you want, I can kick off a full v3 run now and report exactly which v3 files get produced.

Completed — I audited and ran the v3 process end-to-end, and it now maps to v3 folders as intended.

## What I changed to enforce v3 mapping

- Updated `scraper_registry_v3.py` so each v3 scraper config now tracks:
  - `source_output` (where legacy scrapers currently write, usually `output/...`)
  - `output` (canonical v3 path in `output_v3/...`)
- Updated `master_runner_v3.py` to:
  - mirror each successful scraper output from `source_output` -> `output_v3/...`
  - snapshot those mirrored v3 files with date suffixes
  - stream scraper output (removed `capture_output`) to avoid deadlock/hangs
  - remove stale v3 CSV on scraper failure so merge won’t silently reuse old data

## Full v3 run result

I ran `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 python3 master_runner_v3.py` to completion.

- **Exit code:** `0`
- **Duration:** ~18 min
- **Scrapers:** 27 enabled
- **Status:** 25 ok, 2 error
  - `discoverhalifax` failed (`Playwright TimeoutError` on `discoverhalifaxns.com`)
  - `halifaxlive` failed (`Playwright` page load issue on `halifaxlive.ca/shows/`)

## v3 outputs confirmed

These were written in the v3 lane:

- `output_v3/master_events_v3.csv`
- `output_v3/events_archive_v3.csv`
- `output_v3/ready_to_import_v3/new_events_v3.csv`
- `output_v3/ready_to_import_v3/master_events_v3.csv`

Per-scraper outputs and dated snapshots are now present under `output_v3/` as well.

## Important note

Before the stale-file cleanup patch, failed scrapers could still be represented in merge via older mirrored files. That’s now guarded against for future runs.

If you want, I can next fix the two failing v3 scrapers (`discoverhalifax`, `halifaxlive`) so the lane is fully green.

---

_**User**_

is there any skip logic in the process to skip scrapers that have run recently and haven't had any changes to their code?  that might be too complilcated of a request and potentially unnecessary once we get the kinks worked out 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Short answer: **no**, not currently.

`master_runner_v3.py` runs every enabled scraper each time; there isn’t built-in “skip if unchanged/recently run” logic yet.

A lightweight version would be pretty doable later, for example:

- track per-scraper `script_mtime` + last successful run timestamp in a small state file
- skip when:
  - scraper code unchanged
  - output file already exists and is fresh (e.g. within N hours)
- add `--force` to run everything anyway

You’re also right that this is probably unnecessary until stability is locked in. Once the failing sources are green, adding this optimization is straightforward.

---

_**User**_

right now the hfx_editor_blurb is populating incorrectly and usually pulling the venue.  I think since the blurb is supposed to come from me, that field can be skipped in the scraping process

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Makes sense — I’ll stop auto-populating `hfx_editor_blurb` so it stays blank unless you explicitly provide it, then regenerate the v3 master output to confirm.

_**Agent (default)**_

Perfect call — I changed it so `hfx_editor_blurb` is no longer scraper-derived.

### What I changed
- In `merge_master_events_v3.py`:
  - Removed alias mapping from `EVENTORGANIZERNAME` -> `hfx_editor_blurb`
  - Stopped auto-filling `hfx_editor_blurb` from event description
  - Now it only keeps an explicitly provided `hfx_editor_blurb` value (otherwise blank)

- I also mirrored the same behavior in `merge_master_events.py` for consistency.

### Verification
- Re-ran `python3 merge_master_events_v3.py`
- Checked `output_v3/ready_to_import_v3/master_events_v3.csv`:
  - `rows: 483`
  - `hfx_editor_blurb` non-empty rows: **0**

So from now on, that field is effectively reserved for your manual/editorial input.
